points.text: Monte Rosa Therapeutics (Nasdaq: GLUE) is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing a pipeline of first-in-class and only-in-class molecular glue degrader (MGD) medicines spanning autoimmune, inflammatory, and oncology indications with three programs currently in the clinic.

SEED Therapeutics is advancing ST-01156, an orally administered, selective RBM39 molecular glue degrader, in a first-in-human Phase 1 study for patients with advanced solid malignancies, specifically focusing on RBM39-dependent cancers.

SEED Therapeutics presented trial design and preclinical rationale for ST-01156 at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting (Abstract TPS3164) to characterize safety, pharmacology, and early activity signals in patients who have exhausted standard treatment options.

SEED Therapeutics — ST-01156 — Advanced solid malignancies (RBM39-dependent) — First-in-human Phase 1 study ongoing; presented trial design and preclinical rationale at ASCO 2026 (Abstract TPS3164) to evaluate safety, pharmacology, and early activity.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Molecular Glue Degrader (MGD) Pipeline — Autoimmune, inflammatory, and oncology indications — Three programs currently in the clinic; leveraging QuEEN™ discovery engine for rational design of selective MGDs.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Clinical-stage MGD development — QuEEN™ platform and three clinical programs — Developing first-in-class and only-in-class molecular glue degraders for serious diseases; management participating in TD Cowen and Jefferies conferences in May/June 2026.

SEED Therapeutics — Rationally designed molecular glue degraders — ST-01156 (RBM39 degrader) and six total molecular glue programs — Co-founded by Nobel Laureate Avram Hershko; utilizing RITE3™ platform to target undruggable proteins in oncology, neurodegeneration, and immunology.

ST-01156 — Mechanism: Selective RBM39 molecular glue degrader (oral, brain-penetrant) — Phase: 1 (First-in-human) — Population: Advanced solid malignancies (RBM39-dependent cancers) — Comparator: N/A (dose escalation/expansion) — Objective: Rigorously characterize safety, pharmacology, and early activity signals.

Monte Rosa MGDs — Mechanism: Small molecule protein degraders targeting neosubstrates via QuEEN™ engine (AI-guided chemistry, structural biology, proteomics) — Phase: 3 programs in clinic — Population: Patients with serious autoimmune, inflammatory, and oncology diseases — Differentiation: Unprecedented selectivity compared to other degrader modalities.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Investor Relations — Management participation in TD Cowen 7th Annual Oncology Innovation Summit (May 27, 2026) and Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference (June 4, 2026) to discuss MGD pipeline progress.
